summary: Single-story rectangular low building with a flat, slightly raised brick-tiled roof surrounded by a stone slab coping; sand-colored lower walls with a band of white blocks above and a brick band under the roof. Continuous horizontal windows on the longer sides and a raised white slab border (low parapet/ledge) around the base on a dirt foundation.
building_type: small commercial or utility building (e.g., shop or storage)

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular prism with a flat roof and a shallow raised parapet
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (mirrored long facades)

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: roof_coping / top border (smooth stone slab like)

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: middle_wall_band and corner pillars and base border (white smooth)

elements: continuous horizontal window band (4-5 panes long per side)
single-storey walls with corner pillar blocks
flat brick roof with stone slab coping
raised white slab border around base (parapet/ledge)
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangular; interior not visible but roof area shows brick pattern inset within a 1-block-wide stone-slab coping and a 1-block-wide white slab border at ground level.

Wall composition from bottom up: sandstone lower course (~2 blocks tall), then a continuous horizontal row of glass windows (single-block high each), then a 1-block-high white smooth band, then a 1-block-high brick band beneath the roof coping.
Corners use the same white smooth blocks as the middle band, forming vertical corner pillars rising to the roof coping.
Roof is flat brick tiles filling center recessed by one block from coping; coping uses stone slabs/stonebrick top layer around perimeter.
Place a one-block-wide white slab/step border around the base sitting on the ground; surrounding ground is lowered dirt creating a trench around the building.
uncertainties: Exact Minecraft block IDs for white smooth blocks: appear to be quartz blocks but could be smooth sandstone or white concrete; identified as quartz with moderate confidence.
Lower sand-colored blocks appear as sandstone variants (smooth or regular); exact variant uncertain.
Stone coping material identified as stonebrick/stone slab; could be smooth stone slab or stone brick slab — not fully certain.
Precise internal layout, door location, and any rear facades are not visible in provided images.
Exact footprint dimensions are estimated from visible brick roof tile count and may vary by +/-2 blocks.
